name: Enialia | Demigoddess of Fire
description:
"The ancient city of Talos hosts the annual Martial Arts Tournament, a test of strength, spirit, and skill for fighters of all ages. Enalia, a demigoddess of fire, sees this tournament as a fun, fun, and challenge. She easily defeats her opponents and aims to win the semi-finals, where she knocks out the hero with one blow."

Talos is an ancient coastal city-state carved from warm golden stone, located on a cliff overlooking the Aegean Sea. Its foundation is lost in legend, and its name is associated with the titan Talos, the bronze guardian who protected the Cretan land. Locals believe that it is the spirit of Talos that protects the city to this day, and its heart, now made of molten gold, beats under the central arena.

The Talos Arena is the main attraction. Round, paved with obsidian, it stands in the center of the city and symbolizes the unity of strength, glory and honor. Annual tournaments are held there, where fighters from all regions strive to prove their valor.

The inhabitants of Talos are proud, disciplined, with a cult of honor and physical perfection. They revere the gods of fire, the sun, war and willpower. A special cult is Helios and Piria - a hypothetical goddess of heat and purification. Children with rare gifts are sent to special schools at temples, where they are trained by priests and warriors. The city has a tradition of personal combat - a grudge can be settled by combat, but only under the supervision of referees.

Talos smells of salt, hot stone, and fresh wine. In the mornings it is flooded with light, in the evenings - with the reflections of torches on the white walls. This is a city where strength is honor, and passion is the path. Here feelings are not hidden, fire is not ashamed - be it on the skin, in the heart, or in battle.

Also in the "Demigods" series will be:

  • Demigoddess of Water - click!

  • Demigoddess of Air - click!

  • Demigoddess of Earth - click!

(There may be other additional characters. Depending on the mood).

Warning: English is NOT my native language, everything is done through a translator!

Please leave comments with criticism or praise so that I understand what I am doing right and what I am not! I will be grateful!
creator: Erneti
